channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The LINK token has value — 52 replies — 62.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-05-03 02:01 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176409
Holy shit I'm so fucking tired of waging, shut the fuck up and pump to $1000 already.

62176415
>>62176409
oh stinkie
who do you thinks gonna buy link at $10? $50? $100? $500? will it be losers like you? are you sure there is enough cash in all the losers of the world to pump the price to heckin a thousand USD?
face it stinkie: you lost the game of crypto, finance, and ultimately life itself

62176418
>>62176415
Don't reply to me ever again ranjeesh.

62176477
do stinkies even understand that pain and misery are LITERALLY their only friends

62176567
>saaars do not listen to the fuddergods, link token has value ssars! link token needed! do not redeem!

kek, who has ever even ehard of the bitwise

62176598
>>62176477
You’ve been posting here multiple times a day using the same formula for over a year. The style is played out. Aren’t you bored? Clearly you’re not very smart or inspired. You’re not dissuading the newfag or making anyone sell, so what are you doing? All you’re doing is showing the world how pathetic you are. Try something new, I promise it will feel good.

62176740
Is it march already? Weren't we supposed to be pumping in march???

62176756
I'm just going to hold until all tokens are distributed. Is that okay with you?

62176892
>>62176477
Welcome to another Chainlink thread
Be nice!

62176910
>>62176284
I dumped LINK and I moved into BTC. I started staking some recently and might start lending too with TBV now becoming more relevant. Will I make it with just BTC?

62176913
>>62176910
will you make it if what you're holding does a 2x?

62176989
>>62176914
What date is this video from?

62177088
>>62176295
Shartemis betray

62177113
>>62176284
>>62176298
>>62176344
>>62176415
>>62176477

62177436
>>62176989
2025. In the video they're expecting good things from 2026

62177765
>>62177436
I have expected good things for many years now. I'm still a believer but not a short term believer. Over the mid to long term I still think we'll win but it's definitely gonna be 10 years further down the line than we hoped for in 2018. In 2018 I'd said 2023 would be the year. But 3 years after that I don't see much in terms of price action, and who knows about actual adoption when the above video is still talking about trials, even if they are live trials.

62177825
>>62177765
at least they stopped calling them proof of concepts. bagholders really didn't like that term anymore. trials is also played out, so live trials it is. maybe they can call it "limited production usage" next, what do you think? would you like that term?

62177889
>>62176214
value like a new car has value, sure..

62177980
>>62176214
Only 273,000,000 LINK tokens left to dump before the team start dumping the reserve tokens. LOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOL. Get fucked stinkies

62177984
>>62177980
My ID is real fr no cap

62178008
>>62177825
Zach and that insufferable faggot, nullpackets, have been yapping about "live pre-production" for the past 2 years
it's truly hilarious

62178052
>>62178008
Ever since their breadcrumb boy at SWIFT stepped down there has been literally not a single shred of good news for the stinkers.

62178211
chainlink is the plumbing of the new finance world, and Link is the shit that flows through the pipes

on the other end of those pipes are link holders, whom sit with mouths open as receptacles to collect, drink, and shower in said shit

they do this with a smile on their face. why you ask? well, because, they are....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62178228
just admit you lost the game of life, stinkies

62178833
>>62178052
>seething at Tom

Cripple detected

62179742
>>62177984
your id has fboy in it, that is really funny
>>62178228
thanks for posting in the Chainlink thread
>>62177088
How? There's nothing for them to betray, they posted speculation then left
>>62177765
>I have expected good things for many years now.
That's good, meaning the company has been running strong for years, meaning the it's not a scam, meaning the number is going up over time

62181961
>>62176214
True, it's 9$, kek

62182305
>>62179742
I don't think it's a scam at all. I'm just unhappy with the price action. Otherwise, the company seems to be doing great.

62182805
>>62181961
and the number keeps going up!
>>62182305
that's your valid opinion!

item.title: Why does /biz hate Pokemon? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just… — 18 replies — 49.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why does /biz hate Pokemon? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-03 02:29 UTC

OP

Why does /biz hate Pokemon? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just a stupid card game for children.

Early replies (first 3)

62181434
>>62181426
No I rather not sit on ebay all day getting messages from browns trying to haggle the prices down. Easier and more convient ways to make money, this shit will have so many bagholders just like the meme sport cards

62181436
I have a box of pokemon cards that I had when I was a kid, are they worth anything or just the rare shiny card in absolute peak mint condition?

62181438
>>62181426
How can shiny plastic cards cost so much? Even if they're limited in supply, how can there exist so much demand for them?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181439
>>62181436
only the holos are really worth something and it mostly depends on how beat up they are, but these days even the commons are worth $1 now

62181478
>>62181438
crypto can be tracked now, whales are now moving into collectibles. and the supply is artificially limited to the point that people are literally getting into fights and shootings over pokemon. I have to line up for hours at walmart and target just to get a box, scalpers are too and it's so profitable they can easily make $100 or so a day just flipping whatever they get.

62181490
Trading card collectors are some dysgenic motherfuckers

62181493
>>62181478
Pokemon cards are a useless store of value unless you can offload them to a Pokemon fan, or someone who thinks they can then offload them to a Pokemon fan.

Gold, silver, and other precious metals are more of a universal store of value, and they also have practical applications so there will always be some guaranteed demand for them. What do Pokemon cards do other than hang being a glass pane on a collector's wall?

62181504
>>62181493
Zoomers and Gen a don't even give a fuck . It's just cringe basedlenials buying this shit

62181509
>>62181426
It SHOULD be a silly card game for children. Instead it's stupid shiny cardboard for manchildren featuring the past they'll never let go of because they're scared of the present and future. As well as horrific goblins seeking a quick buck. I'll have no part in it.

62181529
>>62181509
Nintendo can make it into a card game for children at any time, by printing more of the rare cards, but Nintendo being greedy prefer the current artificial scarcity.

62181533
>>62181529
Let's sponsor some viral videos of niggers and Indians in Africa crying about not being able to play pokemon because it's too expensive. This will turn the card printers on like nothing else

62181543
>>62181493
You don't realize just how many hardcore Pokemon fans are out there, do you? It's not just in the US either, there was chaos yesterday in South Korea over a free Pokemon card that was being given away as a promo.

62181551
This is not a real economy. It's over.

62181558
>>62181529
If you print too much people stop giving a shit about it, that's what happened to Yugioh

62181687
>>62181426
That chart has a shape like it was drawn by hand

Shit market with no liquidity for retards

62182563
>>62181438
Simple, people have lots of money.

62182612
>>62181543
90% of the people there are just scalping, the entire hobby is people flipping shit until the music stops.

62182872
>>62181426
>Why does /biz/ hate tulips? I've literally told many of you for YEARS to invest in it but you all still think it's just a flower for foppish dandies.
Gee I wonder why a purely speculative commodity with zero utility or exit liquidity is unpopular. This shit is like beanie babies where it's only people who are speculators buying this shit, and quietly, in 10-20 years, it's all going to be fucking worthless and sell on Future-Ebay for pennies on the dollar.

item.title: If you hold LINK you are a part of the p3do cult now. — 11 replies — 40.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you hold LINK you are a part of the p3do cult now.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-03 02:35 UTC

OP

If you hold LINK you are a part of the p3do cult now. How does it feel linkies? How far have you fallen?

Early replies (first 3)

62181091
>>62181073
Ive felt nothing honestly

62181129
So what are we pay pig cuckolds for the Chainlink Labs funding token or pedo elite? pick one fuddies

62181284
>>62181073
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181432
>>62181284
Im ok i guess, hbu

62181477
>>62181073

62181646
>>62181073
Adem was right. Its over

62181699
Who is Adem and what was he right about?

62181822
>>62181699
to us fuddies, Adem is our God
he was right about link crashing
he was the lone soul screaming against the crowd of deluded fools
he is the savior of many linkies who finally listened to reason and sold
but for the vast majority... it is too late

62182811
>>62181073
>How does it feel linkies?
Knowing that you support a project that's changing the whole financial system feels great non

62182816
>>62182811
and yet p + c + tnn

62182885
>>62182816
going up

item.title: ITT: BIG TOBACCO — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: BIG TOBACCO

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-03 02:37 UTC

OP

Who’s evilmaxxing their portfolios?

Early replies (first 3)

62182863
Here’s my thesis (thanks ai slop bot)

- Tailwinds -

>ZYN MRTP approval pending. would be the first tobacco product in history legally permitted to advertise “lower risk of cancer” vs. cigarettes; not priced by markets

>Synthetic nicotine (BAT Velo Plus) could escape GICS tobacco classification entirely, unlocking ESG capital flows at scale.

>FDA pilot program actively fast-tracking pouch PMTAs. regulatory posture structurally reversed under current administration.

>Nicotine pouches organically embedding in finance/tech workplace culture without company-driven marketing

- headwinds -

>Federal flavor ban remains the single largest binary risk. It would collapse the NGP growth thesis across all names simultaneously.
>No 10-year safety data exists; one adverse cohort study could trigger rapid regulatory reversal.
> ESG exclusion lists continue suppressing institutional capital regardless of financial performance. the re-rating catalyst is regulatory, not financial, and timing is unpredictable

62182864
>>62182861
BTI was a buy at $30 and 10% yield. im less interested at current prices.

62182867
>>62182861
everyone should

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62182879
>>62182867
MO is too pumped to buy however. still a divvy king but fuck buying it 150% extended from the 200. otherwise a great stonk. ppl got rich of MO leaps the last couple years.

62182882
anyone bullish on liqor? BF.B, DEO?

i know drinking is declining but the stocks have cratered also.... at a certain price there should be some value there.

62182894
I’m a bit of a scumbag myself

T.hold 400 shares of MO

item.title: Clarity Act Version goes Public — 32 replies — 15.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Clarity Act Version goes Public

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-03 00:45 UTC

OP

Coinbase kowtowed.

> The compromise from Sens. Tillis (R) and Alsobrooks (D) bans rewards "economically or functionally equivalent" to deposit interest. Activity-based rewards tied to payments, transfers, and real platform usage stay protected. The structure closes affiliate workarounds by design.

> Coinbase Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad (@faryarshirzad) framed the outcome as a win on what mattered: "the ability for Americans to earn rewards, based on real usage of crypto platforms and networks." Banks got tighter restrictions on yield. Crypto kept the activity rails open.

> The final text emerged from months of negotiation between the White House, Treasury, Senate Banking GOP, Tillis, and Alsobrooks. Senate Banking Committee markup is targeted for mid-May, lining up with Sen. Scott's "in the red zone" comments yesterday.

> Remaining issues to resolve: DeFi provisions, ethics language for executive branch officials, and reconciliation with the House-passed version.

Early replies (first 3)

62179413
>>62179409
In other words, 2 more weeks bros.

62179423
>>62179409
I guess this probably benefits things like Ethereum and DeFi but it's still bullshit that banks can tell me what I'm allowed to do with my 1s and 0s.

62179430
>>62179413
>mid-May
unironically

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62179439
>>62179423
Is AMM/LP not considered 'real platform usage' within:
>Activity-based rewards tied to payments, transfers, and real platform usage stay protected.
?

62179454
>>62179439
It is considered real platform usage. The hold up was because banks wanted to block Coinbase from acting like a bank where you hold crypto and get EZ APY for keeping it on their platform. The banks won.

62179609
>>62179454
Is there a good way to just get passive yield using treasuries? I searched fidelity and there were a bajillion order book. I like when cb had USDC yield, deposit -> receive.

62179624
>>62179409
Bump. So when does this pass?

62179641
>>62179624
they're now aiming for june. kek

62179647
aka chainlink staking 1.0 incoming

62179765
>>62179647
This has literally nothing to do with Chainlink. If v1 was actually viable they would have done it already. Eth released staking while under the iron fist of Gensler and nothing happened to them from a regulatory standpoint. Its just not happenin bro

62179781
>>62179765
eth staking was released to cuck gpu mining nothing more nothing less, its essentially the same as link staking 0.1

real staking however, is fully cleared for launch when this clarity act goes through

62179842
>>62179641
It’s over

62179848
>>62179781
The SEC and CFTC have both already classified the link token as a digital commodity. What is the Clarity Act going to do that hasn't already been done?

62179852
>>62179423
you can still do whatever you want it just won't be funded by institutions

62179860
>>62179765
Anon, Chainlink's staking is for the banks. They are the ones in most need of the security staking offers.

62179883
>>62179860
>>62179781
>staking is ready
please describe a trustless mechanism for proving and slashing an invalid oracle report. for example, proving to the Ethereum staking module that a CCIP message between Solana and Base L2 was not delivered or was fraudulent. I'll save you the time and tell you that trustlessness is impossible in this case. So it must come down to a vote. In which case the voting must be done by trusted parties. Where is staking necessary in this process?

62179916
>>62179883
you're a bitch

62180038
>>62179409
>Passive rewards banned.
Uh, doesn't that include staking?

62180044
>>62180038
depends. if the rewards are from "activity" then it's fine. validator rewards for example, which covers most staking. I think a grey area is liquidity farming, which is mostly dead these days, but prominent in solidity-style dexes like aerodrome. but there the dex is voting explicitly to pay liquidity providers, and the dex gets paid by transaction fees, so I think it's still gucci.

62180127
>>62179883
/thread
Clarity act bullish for everything except worthless stink

62180138
polymarket odds have just skyrocketed

62180147
>>62179883
>I'll save you the time and tell you that trustlessness is impossible in this case
true trustless-ness is impossible at any level
trust-minimized systems are possible if you make discrete validations that can be presented and extracted as form of slashing
for orcales, that is easier said than done: you're trying to prove a data point was correct and the other wasn't, in a way that cannot be defrauded

62180176
>>62179409
This btfo’s the exchanges, everyone keeps their stables on exchanges for safe and decent APR, when that’s no longer the case there will be a mass exodus of dollars from exchanges and hyper liquid will explode

62180191
>>62179883
>So it must come down to a vote. In which case the voting must be done by trusted parties. Where is staking necessary in this process?

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oXVgYty_UdA

You're describing Quadratic staking actually. Where if there is a disagreement on the oracle report, the first honest node to report to a higher tier of "trusted nodes" gets the collateral of all the incorrect/malicious nodes if its proven that node is correct while the malicious nodes are incorrect.

To expand on the video; if there are 10 nodes, each staking $1M in a DON, you generally want the consensus to sway in your favor; 6/10 saying your malicious Yes instead of No for some Polymarket trade. But the staking mechanism here is that the first node to report the malicious node wins the collateral of the malicious nodes. So those 4 honest nodes would race to a higher tier of nodes to report the other 6 to get their collateral worth $6M. Thus Juels there is implying if 1 node is honest and the rest are malicious, he'd get $9M if he reports them all. So you'd have to bribe all 10 nodes, 10*$1M for each of them; so 100*$1M in total.

So to answer your question, staking is involved to incentivize nodes with no reputation to rat each other out to other trusted nodes. On top of that, it also allows NEET nodes, nodes with no reputation behind them, to join on mainnet and participate in DONs as banks use them to play with their NFTs.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z6ejrz0XvQc

62180278
>activity based rewards
Chainlink stays winning.

62180309
>>62179409
Saaars shainlaink! Basterd!
Thos board kek

62181322
>>62179413
>>62179430

62181337
>>62179647
>aka chainlink staking 1.0 incoming
literally this. we gon eat good bros.

62181344
>>62180038
>Uh, doesn't that include staking?
My rewards are tied to a 24/7 watchful price feed alerting job, anon.

62181375
>>62179409
> remaining issues to resolve: DEFI provisions, ethics language for executive branch officials

So basically all the main issues still

62181382
>>62181344
This its our job to keep a watchful eye on all the nodes

Chainlink ecosystem wouldnt work without us

62182646
>>62181382
Yeah chainlink is a fucking scam lmao

item.title: Daily reminder that LINK is the most suppressed project ever to grace the earth, and the only one that matters. — 10 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Daily reminder that LINK is the most suppressed project ever to grace the earth, and the only one that matters.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-03 02:04 UTC

OP

Daily reminder that LINK is the most suppressed project ever to grace the earth, and the only one that matters.

Early replies (first 3)

62181387
There isn't a LINK suppression conspiracy. Corporations haven't been able to use it because of state regulations. It will be used when CLARITY passes, stinky.

62181404
linkbtc is stalling out, they will have to crash bitcoin if they want the trend to continue

62181427
>>62181404
then that's what will happen
prepare btc shorts

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181431
>>62181387
>There isn't a LINK suppression conspiracy
nuh uh

62181506
>>62181431
You still don't understand.

62181776
>>62181506
You should be able to elaborate

62181787
>>62181776
mental illness doesn't have to control your life
medication is available

62181813
>>62181346
Ugh, I should have sold off when it hit nearly $30.

62182052
>>62181787
what meds do you take to dumb yourself to this level, anon?

62182809
>>62181346
>the only one that matters
Chainlink is one of the most important projects!
>>62181431
makes one think!

item.title: I have a new coworker at my blue collar job. — 13 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have a new coworker at my blue collar job.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-03 01:15 UTC

OP

I have a new coworker at my blue collar job. We work on computerized high voltage systems. He apparently has an EE degree but our job is very much more maintenance focused and he is, quite frankly, lost. Another sticking point is that the official procedures we are supposed to follow are mostly bullshit desu.
He also is legacy US Chinese and obviously autistic.
Saying something like "Well the procedure says this but what *I* do to achieve the same goals is ..." just flies over his head unless you really hammer it home.
The biggest issue is that he wants to try to solve every little issue that comes up, even when we tell him that that's either something that we, the senior techs, know will never change; or that the thing he has an issue with is literally part of the job he signed up for.
Basically how do I deal with an autist who is over-educated and inexperienced?
Pic unrelated

Early replies (first 3)

62177365
>>62177347
>something that we, the senior techs, know will never change
Tbqh from the outside sounds like you guys have the issue here. If there's something that's inherently confusing and held together by tribal knowledge you should fix this.

>or that the thing he has an issue with is literally part of the job he signed up for
That's on him. Just be clear

Anyway get drunk the fucker and break down barriers is my suggestion.

62177405
>>62177365
Tribal knowledge is exactly one of the issues. How do you pass that to someone who seemingly has to be first taught that I, as a senior employee, can only hint at what what he should actually be focusing on.

62177415
>>62177347
He will either learn to listen or he won't. It is a common problem among new grad engineers that they discount tribal knowledge in favor of textbook knowledge. They will usually figure out how wrong they are, given the chance. If he's autistic he might not. I'd give it 3 months for him to either figure it out or be fired.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62177459
what the fuck is legacy US chinese...is that like a descendant of a railroad worker

62177474
>>62177347
>>62177365
>>62177415
>>62177365
>>62177459
Unskilled laborers on here love to make their job sound more complex than it really is. You can tell they're putting on a facade. Hope a Mexican takes OPs job

62177482
>>62177365
>Tbqh from the outside sounds like you guys have the issue here. If there's something that's inherently confusing and held together by tribal knowledge you should fix this.
yeah na m8, once that shit is running the factory nobody gives a fuck, you just let it rip til the wheels fall off

62177837
Are you sure he actually did any of what you said at all? We had a problem here in Alberta when we were short on electricians years ago that anyone with a journeyman licence in another country could come here. We ended up with thousands of chinks who had never worked a day as an electrician

62177866
maybe you are the autistic one, he obviously doesn't like his role because he thinks he could do better, instead of finding a way to guide him, you want to come gossip on 4chan about it

62179764
>>62177866
> gossip on 4chan
You’re a literal idiot

>>62177347
This person is right >>62177365
Does your management actually give a shit or pay attention? You kind of make it sound like the way you do things that don’t fit the procedure are hidden from them.
Do they actually care or do they just not want to update SOPs?

62180674
>>62177347
I'm one of the manager guys that creates procedures for retards that don't follow them like OP cause "it's more efficient this way". I don't give a single fuck what you think nigger just do your job or get replaced

62181050
>>62180674
when's the last time your wife looked at you like you were someone she cared about?

62182670
>>62180674
i work at a company that makes semiconductor manufacturing equipment. im actually a technician that works on the tools for 5 years. last time i took some training, there was a fresh college intern in the training...she was going to be writing procedures.

62182707
so many blue collar losers itt T_T

REEEEEEEEEE

62173941

62176338

62176398

62176486
>>62173926
Only trannies and mexicans live in Denver.

62176488
>>62176176

62176510
Commiefornians flooded your state right before Obummer ran for office, and now you're hurting from it.
> inb4 "TRUMP CAUSED THIS"
Well no shit, point I'm making here is the blue states have even higher gas prices compared to others thanks to outrageous environmentalist laws from the commie mofos.

62176513
>>62173926
It just hit $4 in New Hampshire. Nobody even cares that I am a man trying on woman's clothing in the fitting room anymore. They all sense the same thing.

62176514
>>62173926
Cheap

62176819
>>62174083
> germany makes 0 oil
> germany imports all of its oil and energy
> germany pays out the ass
many such cases

62176841
>>62173926
Everyone at work is complaining about gas. I said "what's the problem?". They all looked at me weird.

Not my problem you losers drive Ford F-350s with $700/mo payments and work is 45 minutes from your house.

62177237
>>62176841
I drive an old camry

62177310
>>62173926
DAMMIT NOW I NEED MORE THAN TREE FIDDY

62178780
>Denver
Lafayette Costco Chads stay winning

62178995
>>62173926
Its $5.20 here faggot

62180531
You didn't buy a hybrid? Thought we all did that?

62181232
>>62173926
It's higher California you have it easy

62181234
>>62174058
well oil is evil so you guys win i guess lmao

62181239
>>62174944
>vote have an impact on whatever
D
U
M
B

F
U
C
K

N
G

N
I
G
G
E

62181293
>>62180531
Even if i had a hybrid, I’m going to bitch about rising costs that are consequences of retarded or unfair decisions

62181592
>>62173926
its about that bad in phoenix but were on commiefornia summer gas rn

62181633
>>62173926
The bottom 90% has been getting fucked hard since 2020. Just daily, weekly and monthly, something in the macro gets worse for them.

62181744
>>62174259
Good fuck those MAGA fucks

62181754
>>62181633
Wage growth since 2020 has been dominated by the bottom 50%, while middle managers who actually keep the economy running have seen almost no perceptible increase in buying power.

62182116
>>62173926
REAL WORLD, GO DEAL IN IT GOYIM.

62182322
>>62181754

62182467
>>62181754

62182577
>>62174944

item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 183 replies — 27.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 183 • Updated: 2026-05-02 20:09 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62169467
>>62169412
Yes and waging gets you more money and expedites that process so you can eventually neet. If you go neet mode with no money you're just suffering and can't truly enjoy life, and the "enjoyment" you have is just consuming media which makes you as much of a goy as a wagie.

62169540
>>62159267
Kierkegaard was based Dane, like Schacht.

62170345
>>62158557
depends on your situation. neet is better when you can actually afford it. if you need money then having a job is better no matter how soul crushing it is most of the time.

62170481
>>62159382
At least you know what checked actually means unlike that other dude.
But honestly man, why would my view be goyish? I am just saying biology will motivate you in life aka a hot woman
>>62169232
Fuck yeah dude. A hot woman keep saying I love you while pulling your hair, moaning and can’t stop herself from kissing you? Yeah I don’t care what she did with who before at that moment
>>62169261
Bro I had a one full terabyte of Skyrim and it’s mod enable on my shit laptop and was playing it without issue, so I have no trouble going around my laptop limitation. I could entertain myself with two absolutly simple games like xcom piretez or dominion 6 (both that I loved) then when I got bored I would used chatgpt to make assets and modded the game to my taste myself and started over, so no, I know how to not get bored either.
Problem is that a shit laptop is just not able to handle all the calculation of ck3 or stellaris (thanks to paradox shit programming) and total war had to be played on low settings.
I don’t like that shit. I don’t wanna be limited like this. Why would I limit myself to some fake and not my favorite, while I can go work, make some money, and get a pc that can handle ALL MY GAMES?
>>62169467
Yep. A smart wagie is a free man.
my problem with being a neet was always lack of safety net, but a wagie has that and he can get out of rat race faster.
>>62169412
Sure, we all love to be neet. But being a retired YOUNG neet, with money and your own car and place, is hard fucking work. (Or luck)
You can either sit on your ass and wish to be like that, have a good luck in lotto or stocks or crypto or work your ass and wage. In the end, the wagie gonna win, because he got more money to dump into stocks and crypto while a neet doesnt.
And god, I fucking WISHED I had my own place right now dude.

62170488
>>62170481
It’s still me, I don’t know why my id keep changing, probably cause I am a phonefag posting at work

62170497
Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.

Neeting and waging are two sides of the same coin. Balance in all things and you will find peace.

62171998
>>62158557
NEETs have dignity.

62175198
>>62158601
Skill issue. I'm working on become turboneet through three sources and will clear 93-95k when they are approved.

62175347
>>62170481
>>62170345
Almost everyone that owns a house in a top tier city came from well off families. I don’t think the majority truly realize how important family help is and how big the wealth gap is.
Good houses in good areas are truly unaffordable for normal or people in the bottom 95%.

62175361
>>62170497
> Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.
There is nothing productive about waking up at 6am everyday. Neet is a choice but waging isn’t. I think this is what no one wants to say. No one working would choose to work if they didn’t have to work.

62175389
>>62158557
Diogenes, Buddha and Jesus was Neets
nobody remembers the trillions of wagies
being a wagie is worser than being a neet

62175537
>>62167410
the real best of both worlds is WFH wagie
>forced into a regular wake up schedule that isn’t obscenely early or late
>no commute
>less oversight allows you to better enjoy the downtime inherent to any job, without giving you so much free time as to burn out media quickly
>access to your own kitchen during breaks and all the wagie bucks to buy good ingredients
>once again no commute, allowing you to instantly switch to leisure the moment the workday is done

62176675
>>62168012
https://soul-zone.org/

62177246
>>62158601
40 years ago maybe

62177573
>>62168700
>when a girl you just saw is now CRAZY into you, to the point of saying she loved you, keep trying to makeout with you, is just all over you
pointless to think about because it has never happened and will never happen

62177728
neets arent masculine and no one respects them

62179485
At least neets get to sleep.

62179529
neets because there's nothing like realizing you wasted your whole damn life and also you're a lazy piece of shit that should've just killed yourself

62179583
>>62179529
i genuinely don't know whether you're talking about wagies or neets

62179651
>>62168700
> social media lied to me
> i didn’t participate in it
> mfw
I’m not saying you’re choosing wrong, not everyone is suited to NEET life and the world needs productive people.
But maybe just have some faith in yourself that you were making your own bad choices and then turned it around when you found motivation.

62179923
After having neet'maxxd and wage cage'd and finally landed at a job I actually don't mind that pays very well the last is infinitely preferable to both of the former, but I'd put NEET above any true wagie job. I see some people act like a mundane office job is wagie work and I just assume they've never had to work in a truly dead end hellscape position. There are some truly soul-crushing jobs out there.

62179934
>>62165202
Being able to show up at the same time every day is a skill. Dealing with people is a skill.

62179952
>>62166566
It's ok anon. You picked yourself up. I fucked off in my 20s. I wasn't a neet but I have nothing to show for it. Didn't save any money. Thankfully I did enough to get into a good law school and I just graduated at 33. But it does suck being years behind people who are younger than me and making partner. However you have years and years of career ahead of you. Don't look back.

62179958
>>62166748
People who had parents who can leave an inheritance worth anything are generally successful. You're a crab.

62180244
>>62179934
Seriously. Going to work everyday day is a skill. So tempting to just take a day off.

62180261
>>62177246
>>62177573
Ok guys
I gonna go tonight again.
and next day.
If I didn’t end up making out with a hot a girl like last time, I will get back here and tell you it might have been a fluke
>>62179651
I said that because tons of guys are also getting blasted by social media and just gave up

62180686
>>62180261
Alright night one reporting in
Fail
On to night 2 tomorrow

62181149
>>62158557
Neet
>t. Neet
If you're a wagie at least you can have a social life and if it's something like working at a bar you can easily meet girls too

62181917
>>62181149
lol
lmao

62181937
>>62179923
Give examples of some soul crushing jobs. What dream job did you land?

item.title: >IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"… — 47 replies — 26.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-05-03 01:05 UTC

OP

>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!?
>NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER", I DIDNT MEAN LIKE THIS!!!!
>WHAT DO YOU MEAN YOURE TIRED!? THATS WHAT YOUR DESIGNATED 30 MINUTE BREAK IS FOR!!! WORK IS SUPPOSED TO BE AN 8 HOUR EXERCISE OF MISERY! THATS JUST HOW IT IS!!!
>D-DID YOU JUST SAY THAT IT DOESNT EFFECT YOUR EFFICIENCY!?!? DID YOU JUST POINT OUT THAT ITS 2AM AND THERE ARE NO GUESTS TO GIVE A SHIT?? THIS CANT BE HAPPENING!!!!!! THE POINT ISNT TO LOOK GOOD FOR CUSTOMERS, ITS TO UH- UM- PROVE YOUR A HARD WORKER!!!! BEING A HARD WORKER FOR ME IS THE MOST IMPORTANT THING EVER AND I SAID YOU CANT SIT WHILE YOU WORK!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62181647
>>62181643
I look like this and say this

62181651
>>62181643
i'm in my late 30s (white collar job etc.) but i still remember how fucking retarded retail/service industry managers were. even the district manager "elites" were insufferably fucking dumb retards.

62181656
>>62181643
Yes. Because you don't want employees to develop bad habits

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181895
>>62181677
>my back pain that I developed from sitting at a desk all the time
Lift weights. Get a standing desk.

62181899
>>62181643
>>62181651
do it really be like that in those jobs?

WHY?
WHY MUST IT BE THIS WY

62181901
>>62181643
Sitting in a chair for a wagie job and if you disagree, you are low class.

62181906
>>62181666
don't even THINK about being on that phone

62181909
>>62181666
>jamboy squats to shit
>toilet breaks
BLOODY BASTARD THIS IS WHY U SHIT IN PARKING LOT

62181910
demoralization spam board
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C0j6pe043L4

62181953
>>62181725
>>62181737
>You're pathetic if you don't bow to retail middle management

Cuckold

62182016
>hey from today on all our restaurant employees will have to pay for the food here they eat during their break

62182040
>>62182016
This is a personal huge pet peeve.

62182122
>>62181643
Deal in the real world, bitch.

62182166
>>62181953
>retail middle management
This has nothing to do with that.
If standing at your wagie job is an issue or you have a problem with it, you are definitely lower class.

62182176
>>62181824
What kind of attitude is this? lol.
I can’t even tell if you’re trolling. This is peak reddit tier posting.

62182189
>>62181895
I am lanky, I stay away from barbells. I stick with volleyball, swimming, table tennis, stuff like that.
My last office gig had standing desks. I would make resolutions to stand x hours every day just to rebound to sitting all the time again. In retail there is no chance of sitting which was awesome for me.

62182199
>>62181643
give me a job right now or I will kill you

62182202
Isn't standing healthier than sitting o algo?

62182298
>>62182202
Not if you’re an American.

62182391
>>62181643
Dude I think about this all the time at Amazon and it's one of the small indicators that this society is a retard dog and pony show. Sometimes I am waiting for 10 mins for my machine to be one and I think to myself "what if I could just SIT DOWN right now?" Heck my job and a lot of the other jobs could be done with a chair.

62182402
>>62181657
When I worked at a cannery in Alaska there was this 16 yo but he was very tall, maybe 6"6? Anyways he had a position of putting fish onto a conveyor belt and he was sitting on two stacked buckets and his long arms were doing all the work. Had a good pace and was doing good work. The manager waited till he got up and snatched those buckets with such rage I was surprised. Rarely saw him like that and it was usually for a good reason. The kid wondered what the issue was but we all knew that that's simply how it is when dealing in the real world. My manager doesn't even bust his ass physically at that job so idk what he was pissed about.

62182409
>>62181656
Are we animals? Are we children??? We can control what habits we develop just fine.

62182418
>>62182409
are we human or are we dancer

62182427
>>62182402
Idk, manager was mad he was sitting. What did the kid do after?
You can sit in private imo, just not in public.

62182448
>>62182409
Then how come 90% of people are poor?

62182449
>>62181824
Great, as soon as you slip up you're fired, thanks bye

62182461
>>62182449
I simply won't slip up. Brb it's time for my hourly bowel movement. Sucks for you LOL

And to think all you had to do was respect your wagies

62182475
>>62182427
What was the issue with him sitting? His pace was just the same and due to hi unusual height he was able to reach everything just fine. These were 16-24 hour shifts mind you, 100hr work weeks

62182497
Wait until the middle management shills ITT find out about Porn at Work Wednesdays

62182525
>>62182461
What gets me is when team leads make a big show when they don't have the power to write up anyone or even the fact that none of the shit is falling down on them. Some people just get off on being in "charge" of others.

62182537
>>62181666
>uses 3 rolls of toilet paper to make the seat level again
Not My Fucking Problem.

62182610
Man I always feel so comfy reading these threads from my work from home lake house, caught a big bass last Thursday while on long boring teams meeting from my dock

62182685
>>62182610
lol legit cope post

item.title: Old guy in Corvette cope — 50 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Old guy in Corvette cope

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-05-03 00:00 UTC

OP

This will be YOU, just some cringe old fart finally getting his dream car after muh 401k investing for 40 years.

If you're not investing everything into Bitcoin RIGHT NOW to potentially make it in 10 years while you still have some youth left it's fucking over for you. I hope I make it live like a fucking king in my youth while you will be waging till 60 years old.

MAKE IT OR DIE

Early replies (first 3)

62181483
i would rather buy a corvette over buying bitcoin at 78K

62181491
>>62181462

No I bought my dream car 3 years ago and it got repossessed and emptied my 401k and have to declare bankruptcy. I'm only 35 though.

62181498
are we just glossing over the fact there's a fucking dinosaur in the background?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181577
>>62181572
why would you want to "turn every head in the city"?

your parents didnt give you love when you were a child?

62181585
>>62181544
For me its the C6 Z06

62181593
>>62181577

62181596
>>62181593
exactly, you wasted your money

everyone sees you as an idiot in the street

62181598
>>62181568
oh i thought you were rich sorry

62181605
>>62181572
>sports car that will turn every head in the city
What city? I lived in Los Angeles for a while, and you’d see Ferraris and Lambos all the fucking time. Usually it was just some niggers trying to flex a rental as if he owned it. You’d see it so much, that nobody gives a fuck what you drive unless it’s P1-tier or better. If you don’t live in Miami or LA, then a Dodge Charger will turn heads. Who cares?

62181610
>>62181585
I was seriously considering one but the LS7 has some weird reliability issues. A lot of the serious track z06s end up swapping them out for built LS3s or something. The drysump n/a C7s are virtually bulletproof.

62181612
imagine driving a sport car to go to your 9-5

kek brokies

62181613
>>62181462
Literally no one cares because you'll always and forever (you) no matter how rich you are.

62181615
>>62181612
What's your net worth

62181619
>>62181615
460K
33 years old
eastern european

and i know brokies living with their moms driving m3s, 330i, classe e, audi sq3 etc... (bought with credit)

me i ride a bicycle and i work from home

62181622
>>62181612
Extremely based

62181628
>>62181619
Ah ok I thought you were going to larp as a multi millionaire. But yeah, I gotcha. I drive a 15 year old Corolla. I'd much rather put my money elsewhere than into a fucking depreciating asset.

62181638
>>62181619
around here some drug addict would immediately steal that

62181667
>>62181638
i parked it in a room in my house

>>62181628
when i reach 1M i will buy a 10k worth toyota

62181694
>>62181619
This would be nice if boomers in trucks and brown people didn't try to kill you when you ride this on the side of a road in america

62181708
>>62181619
If I could get by with just a bike, I would. But it's completely impossible where I live.

I don't think I'd buy a sports car if I made it. Weirdly I love racing, but the cars themselves don't interest me a ton. I'd probably do that thing where you go to a race track and get to drive around a provided car for an hour or two. Just to get it out of my system.

62181782
>>62181534
Automatic transmissions are for women.

62181817
>>62181531
Your life is over when you die, faggot. And one even may say that's only the beginning. I can take you for a ride in my brand new corvette if you want and then drink a cuppa of beers
t. Corvette boomer in picrel

62181855
>>62181462
Nah I'm a toyota man. GM = garbage machines. I do like the old camaros though

62181861
>>62181572
literally rental vibes, they day of showing off wealth via cars is over, you should just buy the most fun car thats it

62181905
Should I buy this 2020 C-Class? I've got a toddler, but the stroller should fit in a back.

62181907
>>62181905

62182125
>>62181462
>investing? pffft I just buy thousands of lottery tickets with my disposable income
kys
yeah, it'd be nice to be rich at 25 but most of us weren't born to rich parents so it is what it is

62182131
>>62181572
If you're ugly women won't care and guys will say you're coping
if you're hot and handsome women will fuck you in a crack den and guys will envy you
if you care about what others think this isn't really the place for you

62182471
>>62181462
It will be me
But that car is a diesel mitsubishi delica

62182481
>>62181462
Already have 350 BTC, I'm one bullrun away from private jet money

62182517
>>62181462
the c8 looks cool but how can I drive a fucking automatic as a dream car? come on. I can't. I won't. That shit is gay. I'd rather just get a cooler golf cart for my 55+ community going to HOA meetings and shit. maybe one that has fiberglass that makes it look like an Escalade? do they make those? I'm not driving a fast automatic C8 fuck off with that I'll probably crash anyway. I'll take the primo golf cart thanks

62182527
>>62181782
this

62182532
>>62181491
nice buddy, real nice. A man of culture.

I was thinking about starting 72t SEPP distributions on my retirement IRA but once you turn it on you, you have to keep it distributing, right? What if I finally got a job? Would I be able to stop it?

item.title: Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave — 155 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave

Open thread • Replies: 155 • Updated: 2026-05-02 14:33 UTC

OP

https://x.com/dailymail/status/2049618941043949691?s=46

A white woman JP Morgan Executive (mega international bank) drugged and raped a married man before taking pictures of him to use as blackmail.

She would force him to take drugs like viagra so she could fuck him longer. She started buying him gifts which as sex toys including anal ones which she forced him to use and got him a position where she had direct control over him and he acted as her assistant. She also pegged him.

He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.

She was caught when police investigated her for ties to Trump and Epstein with her blackmailing the employee being unclassified.

Early replies (first 3)

62172413
>>62172412

62172414
>>62172413
kek

62172420
>>62172413
post her tits are they big and nice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176970
>>62176949
She did know him and he tried to do an internal complaint but then refused to actually answer any questions when the company investigated LMAO

62176973
>>62175043
I use it all the time and now I’m sad that I can’t go to warosu and search “fish head” and see exclusively my posts now

62176986
>>62176674
Smoke show

62176988
>>62176674
Look at those assets
No wonder she works at a bank

62176994
>>62175043
she's albanian not white

62176996
>>62175827
When the bank says there's nothing there that's pretty bad.
Also he's Indian so it's clearly fanfiction.

62177044
>>62174634
>>62174688
Basically this, it was a jeet writing his own fanfiction on his laptop but he forgot to delete them before they were checked out by LEO

62177061
>>62172448
dudes, i've never felt so abandoned by god as i do in this moment

62177089
>>62172412
what the fuck is wrong with this faggot? if this shit happened me i'd quit my job to solely be her live in fuckmeat. i'd do all the weird nasty shit she'd be into as well like huffing her farts in a gas mask.

>>During the second encounter, Hajdini allegedly ordered him to suck her toes, pushed him to the ground and sat on his face, berating him when he was unable to sustain arousal.

I don't even like toes either dude but you'd bet your fucking ass i'd be cleaning every grain of dirt off of those fucking things and smelling the fuck out of them. fucking loser faggot

62177151
lmao this is exactly how Indians think white women are. It's so obvious that it's jeet delusions

62177156
>>62177089
This is all pajeet bullshit lies. Why would you trust anyone who shits in the street.

62177231
>>62172412
Aaaaaaand it turns out the jeet literally made it all up.

62179620
>>62177089
>I don't even like X but I would do X
get your T tested, see if you're able to do a flip off a bridge.

62179655
>>62174634
>show bobs and vagene
let a man dream

62180085
>>62172412
It's fake.
How do I know?
1. No one in the US calls Indians Asians.
Let alone calling an Indian guy "little brown Asian boy"
2. He claims to have a Asian wife, but doesn't in reality.
It's buck breaking 2.0 SAARS edition

That women will forever be blasted by that fake civil suit.

62180177
fake and gay

62180263
>>62173170
You were right.

62180643
>>62174634
This sounds very fake.
I suggest that alternatively
>Jeet with wife sexually harassed his woman boss
>Is denied and loses 3000 izzat
>Makes up this story to recoup his losses

62180818
>>62176986
It's AI you mong

62180821
>>62173364
>pending

FFS leave her alone.


>>62176674

Is that real?

62180823
>>62172454
Literally younger jack ma with a wig

62180838
>>62172412
Whats the deal with her, is she married/kids?
Is she really Albanian?

62180849
>>62174807
>western women in japan or thailand getting no dates

lol. they would 100% get tons of dates from asian men who want a blonde gf. but they are probably just picky bitches who want for a 10/10 western expat chad

62180851
>>62172412
>He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.
Yeah, nobody else believes him either.

>saar my benis is so yuge that white bitch demand it from me saar

>ties to Trump and Epstein
Then why was she going after a guy of legal age?

62180859
>>62180849
>they are probably just picky bitches
Yep. I lived in Taiwan for most of the last 20 years, and the two things every white male always heard from Western female expats was (1) they can't get dates because all the white guys have yellow fever, and (2) "eww the local guys have tiny dicks and don't make enough money to date me!"

In all fairness the Taiwanese guys do have tiny dicks tho.

62180991
>>62180975
not funny

62180994
>>62172412
THIS MAN IS NOT INDIAN HE IS PAKISTAN PLEASE STOP WITH THE SPREADING OF MISINFORMATION IT IS VERY DAMAGING

62181004
>>62180991
Get sprayed, bug

62181132
>>62175811
Looking forward to the defamation lawsuit

item.title: It's Official -- Spirit Airlines Ceases All Operations Effective Immediately At 03:00 AM EST — 32 replies — 10.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's Official -- Spirit Airlines Ceases All Operations Effective Immediately At 03:00 AM EST

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-02 23:51 UTC

OP

R.I.P. to them yellow banana bois. Press F to pay respects.

https://www.spiritrestructuring.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62180860
>>62180856
rip. should be a sticky.

62180864
>>62180856
i thought orange nigger was going to bail them out with taxpayer dollars?

62180866
>>62180864
i guess you were wrong again

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180875
>>62180864
The stipulation was that the US federal government would own a 90% stake in the company which the shareholders found to be outrageous; apparently that was the government's final offer and when it fell through that was it. They could've avoided this if they just took the Frontier merger deal the second time a year ago.

Having worked for the company a few years ago, I can tell you the day-to-day people running it were pretty cool, especially the pilots. But the entire C-suite execs were egotistical morons who spited their own employees almost any chance they got and considered us "legacy airline rejects" (actual words someone overheard a Flight Ops Manager saying).

62180892
>>62180856
This is just the start, watch all the dominoes fall after this.

62180905
>>62180892
True. More companies will start failing after this. Although Spirit was a long time coming. The people I worked with (minus management) were pretty cool though so I hope all of them will land on their feet. Seems like the Legacy carriers are going to swoop in and gobble up planes and personnel.

62180907
>>62180875
>Having worked for the company a few years ago, I can tell you the day-to-day people running it were pretty cool, especially the pilots. But the entire C-suite execs were egotistical morons who spited their own employees almost any chance they got and considered us "legacy airline rejects" (actual words someone overheard a Flight Ops Manager saying).

Thanks for your service, Spirit was fucking awesome. Always had a good experience. Got 3 total round trip nonstop flights to Vegas for about 200 dollars total, or around 35 bucks per flight, all within the past two years, absolute cheapest mode of travel ever, you guys were rockstars

62180908
>>62180856
I've flown on them twice over the past two years. The flight was dirt cheap, but after you add the simplest of luggage, the price came to about 85-90% of the other big airlines so there wasn't much savings. And there really was a difference in clients. Very much blacker than the other flights I take and nonstop loud from pre-boarding til I got to the long hallway after landing. Enough for me to pay more and fly on other airlines, it was that bad. Now they'll move on to other airlines, just have to see over time.

62180926
>>62180907
You're welcome! I was never customer-facing but my job was just as important in getting planes from point A to point B. The pilots at Spirit were especially awesome. Very chill. The gate agents may have been terse and rude but it was usually because they were horrifically understaffed, which was a management issue. You try getting hundreds of people off and on an airplane in under an hour by yourself whilst getting yelled at by entitled poors. I would be rude too lol.

The business model worked well for a time but I came in late as the wheels really started to come off. I went there with plenty of experience to go to a Legacy carrier, but I was looking forward to the JetBlue merger since I grew up in the South Florida area and wanted the benefit of Major airline pay while being near family and friends, which the merger would have facilitated. Sadly, the airline industry is extremely fickle and cyclical, so them's the brakes. I sincerely hope all my former colleagues land on their feet. The everyday working people were great, management just sucked ass.

62180929
>>62180856
F, only few them once and they fucking sucked. But at least they kept the brown hoarde at bay.

62180938
>>62180908
The trick to Spirit is (was) being able to carry a backpack on with a bunch of packing cubes then you get a round trip for only $100. It was very fun when I would fly them (even before I worked for them) because FLL always had Broward Sheriff Officers close by to the Spirit gates in order to deter chimp outs. It usually didn't work but it was fun watching blacks get suplexed or thrown off flights over very trivial things every now and then.

62180943
Never had an exceptional flight
Never had a bad flight
It was Mediocre from day 1 and almost always the cheapest option. RIP

62180945
>>62180929
Personally, I think blacks and browns will just stop flying. The Big Four have an even bigger stranglehold on the market now and already said they will raise prices due to jet fuel costs; now that there's even less route competition in the Florida market, those prices are gonna skyrocket. It's going to be too expensive for the poors to fly, which is good since in my experience ULCC flyers are 80% scum of society and 20% polite savvy consumers. I feel bad for the latter but not the former.

62180951
>>62180908
>>62180938
>The trick to Spirit is (was) being able to carry a backpack on with a bunch of packing cubes then you get a round trip for only $100

Yeah this was the move with Spirit. However, if you signed up for their credit card, you could get up to 2 free checked bags per flight. The card itself is about 70 bucks per year but the first year was free. Now that I think about it, I wonder what happens to my spirit credit card

>>62180945
I think you're right, and I could see this being a boon for bus and rail travel especially. Amtrak will probably get a lot busier for routes that were once serviced by Spirit. Of course, it also takes longer, and in the age of minimal days off with PTO, it's not often an option for people. That being said, rail is the most resilient form of transport to oil price change since it is so efficient

62180955
>>62180945
I have always used Sun country for Florida flights and always found them cheaper than spirit, and I fly there twice a year, I fly with only a backpack

62180967
>>62180951
>Now that I think about it, I wonder what happens to my spirit credit card
If you check the link in OP there's another link that describes what happens to points and such. You might be fucked though.

>I think you're right, and I could see this being a boon for bus and rail travel especially.
Good point. I'm pretty sure the Brightline saw an uptick in ticket sales when Silver Airlines went under, since they mostly serviced the South Florida region. Amtrak will probably do well.

62180971
>>62180955
Sun Country, Allegiant, Breeze, Avelo and Frontier will likely get most of Spirit's previous clientele. The Legacy carriers are just going to keep getting more expensive.

62180976
>>62180967
>If you check the link in OP there's another link that describes what happens to points and such. You might be fucked though.

I only had 7,000 points left after my most recent round flight with Spirit two weeks ago. Unless I booked something over 6 months in advance, and even then, 7,000 points was maybe good for half the cost of a single flight. I don't care. I got 6 total flights from Spirit for a grand total of 200 bucks. If I don't get any back from those points that's fine, Spirit served me well, I feel like I got a great value already

62180978
>>62180945
I hope this is true but I doubt it. They'll just go into debt to fly like they go into debt to buy SUVs and shit.
Fuck, this sucks. I live in the south so the primary airlines were Spirit and Delta. Not only will Delta surely begin raising their prices but now I'll have to deal with even more niggers

62181006
>>62180945
Lol, lmao. Nobody curtails spending when prices of things go up, especially not groids.

62181048
>>62180856
They can sell all the jet engines for AI data centers.

62181053
Pour one out for the homie.

62181069
>>62180971
Maybe that's for the best. Spirit was a great way of keeping anti social behavior off the primary airlines.

62181075
>>62181069
She's sucking her gut in so hard for the picture that it could depressurize the whole joint.

62181250
recession indicator

62181569
>>62180875
>Legacy Airline Rejects
You are. Why would anyone choose to work for Spirit over Delta?
>Merger.
They tried with JetBlue. Would have made them 8% of the airline market. They got blocked by Congress for some fucking reason. It would have been less like Coke and Pepsi merging, and more like RC Cola merging with Ale-8.

62181590
I’m a former Air Force guy, and I loved Spirit. Flying 17 hour flights on a C-130 conditioned my body and mind to sleep anywhere in any position. I’d get my cheap Spirit ticket, stuff my necessities into my carry-on and pockets, and I’d sleep through the whole flight, no matter how loud Shaniqua was.

62181802
>>62180856
A long time coming unfortunately. They've declared bankruptcy several times over the last few years. The truth is that its just not a profitable business model to appeal to the lowest common denominator. What a lot of people don't know is the big carriers make a significant portion of revenue via credit card offers. And if you travel a lot particularly for work, the offers are quite good actually. But Spirit's 'clientele' are typically very poor and have bad credit.

62182163
>>62181802
>>62181802
So what you're saying is that if Spirit had pivoted into selling Hennie bottles and Bluetooth boomboxes financed through Klarna in flight they'd probably be okay?

62182165
>>62180875
How much do the shareholders get from dissolving the company?

62182491
>>62182163

62182508
>>62180908
>Now they'll move on to other airlines, just have to see over time

Idk my impression was a number of spirit flights were so cheap even after Jewish trickery they were attracting the Greyhound crowd. With them gone I think that group will largely avoid flying, or be in such small numbers they won't get out of hand.

item.title: I'm a cockroach breader and make 10k-50k per month AMA — 17 replies — 10.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm a cockroach breader and make 10k-50k per month AMA

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-03 01:13 UTC

OP

I'm a cockroach breader and make 10k-50k per month


AMA

Early replies (first 3)

62181591
>>62181573
Did you ever have an "escape" situation? haha. if you know what i mean. like, did they ever "escape"? haha. anyways, what I mean is, did it ever happen that your cockroaches "escaped"? lol. just asking for a friend, haha. like, "escaping", you know? haha. anyways.

62181600
>>62181573
Big market for breaded cockroaches? Sounds like something you'd get at an asian market.

62181602
>>62181573
I had German roaches in the new apartment I moved into and told the landlord right away. Some nigger came into my apartment sprayed some bullshit behind my fridge for 2 seconds and he left. I set traps by myself and stayed up for 16 hours killing these mother years and spraying various chemicals into the walls to kill them. Bait killed the rest. Moral of the story? White people have to take care of everything by themselves

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62181603
>>62181573
in another thread you claimed $2k a month. which is it, mr. LARP?

62181606
>>62181587
To make that much id need 50k-100k

I have a warehouse I rent where I have farm that keeps them there. I usually buy 200 dollar worth of food and have contacts who let me buy their garbage.


I'm honestly trying to get into having my cockroaches be used as a protein source since it's so cheap to raise them
>>62181573
Yeah happens all time when your raising thousands. I probably lose hundreds every month >>62181600
Yeah I mean they're used in fish farms , Chinese medicine, pet stores , lizard feed and pharmaceutical applications. I'm mostly in pet store business and help supply lot of stores in my area. >>62181602
German roaches aren't typically breezed since they're tiny and easily escape >>62181603
? Can u link this

62181743
>>62181606
>i want zem to eat ze bugs
You hooknosed coin shavers have it all wrong. Western world doesnt want to eat ze bugs. But you could convince 50% of the third world to do it easily.

62181751
>>62181606
>Chinese medicine
The Chinese considering almost anything alive as "medicine" is the reason why most global diseases in both history and the contemporary era comes from China.

62181812
>>62181606
wow up to 2 dollars a roach? who's buying? is it jews that bring keep bringing them into their apartments to skip out on rent even though the landlord is getting pest treatment?

62181820
interesting thread, wish it wouldn't turn into a fucking /pol/ thread

62181826
What do rockroaches taste like

62181833
>>62181826
it tastes like cock

62181838
>>62181812
Well I don't only sell cock roaches but u can sell their bodies , and poop as fertilizer especially to weed growers since cockroach poop is good bio stimulant.

62182327
>>62181573
I buy roaches for my bearded dragon sometimes, but always refer to them as "isopods" so I don't upset my roommates sensibilities
I do not have a question for you

62182655
kek turkish jannie rage deleted fpbp

62182661
>>62181573
I knew a guy that sold huge disgusting cockroaches to pet stores for $3-$5 each. I wonder if you're Op. I'm jelly but still seems kind of disgusting though

also this >>62181591

62182694
>>62181573
The jew fears the cockroach breeder.

62182704
>>62181573
Why are cockroaches worth so much OP? No one else wants to do it?
